caption: Detection Finding
description:
A Detection Finding describes detections or alerts generated by security products using correlation engines, detection engines or other methodologies.
Note: If the event producer is a security control, the Security Control profile should be applied and its attacks information, if present, should be duplicated into the finding_info object.
Note: If the Finding is an incident, i.e. requires incident workflow, also apply the Incident profile or aggregate the finding into an Incident Finding.

Note: Findings are mutable and as such have a lifecycle, via Update activities. When modeling atomic alerts, you are encouraged to consider using the Security Control profile with the activity event associated with the alert. See the referenced article below.
name: detection_finding

references.description: How to Model Alerts with OCSF

anomaly_analyses.description: Describes baseline information about normal activity patterns, along with any detected deviations or anomalies that triggered this finding.

detection_system.description: The technology or sensor layer that reported or originated the detection, normalized to the caption of detection_system_id. When detection_system_id is 99 (Other), this attribute must contain the source-specific label.

detection_system_id.description: The normalized identifier of the technology or sensor layer that was involved in, or reported, or originated the detection. Use this to classify the detection surface (e.g., IPS sensor, endpoint agent, email gateway). When the type is not listed, use 99 (Other) and populate detection_system with the source-specific label.

1.caption: Network
1.description: The detection is reported by, or originates from a network, firewall, or IPS sensor.

2.caption: Endpoint
2.description: The detection is reported by, or originates from an endpoint device.

3.caption: Email
3.description: The detection is reported by, or originates from an email gateway or email security system.

4.caption: IAM
4.description: The detection is reported by, or originates from an identity and access management (IAM) system or service.

5.caption: Proxy
5.description: The detection is reported by, or originates from a proxy server (e.g., forward or reverse proxy, web gateway).

6.caption: Threat Intel
6.description: The detection is reported by a cyber threat intelligence (CTI) system or by matching observables against a threat intelligence feed or report.

7.caption: Application
7.description: The detection is reported by, or originates from an application (e.g., web application, SaaS platform, or custom business application).

evidences.description: Describes various evidence artifacts associated to the activity/activities that triggered a security detection.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. For example, an activity_id of 'Create' could constitute an alertable signal and the value would be true, while 'Close' likely would not and either omit the attribute or set its value to false. Note that other events with the security_control profile may also be deemed alertable signals and may also carry is_alert = true attributes.

malware.description: Describes malware reported in a Detection Finding.

malware_scan_info.description: Describes details about malware scan job that triggered this Detection Finding.

resources.caption: Affected Resources
resources.description: Describes details about resources that were the target of the activity that triggered the finding.

vulnerabilities.description: Describes vulnerabilities reported in a Detection Finding.
